name: Whiteman STXDF Hydrostatic-Drive Ride-On Trowel
shortDescription: The MQ Whiteman STXDF is a 84 HP dual-fuel (gas/LPG) hydrostatic ride-on trowel with a 10-foot non-overlap twin six-blade path — purpose-built for high-production commercial and industrial slab finishing. Available from CTA Services for sale, rental, and factory-trained service.
longDescription:
CTA Services LLC supplies and services the MQ Whiteman STXDF Hydrostatic-Drive Ride-On Trowel — one of the most capable concrete finishing machines available for large-scale commercial and industrial slabs.

POWER & DRIVE SYSTEM
Powered by an 84 HP Ford Power Products dual-fuel engine, the STXDF delivers optimum torque for both panning and high-speed burnishing. The dual-fuel capability allows seamless switching between gasoline and LPG — extending run times up to 35% over single-fuel models and enabling operation in semi-enclosed environments. A high-efficiency hydraulic variable displacement pump and radial piston drive motors maintain consistent rotor speeds under varying slab load conditions, reducing stalls and uneven finish. The redesigned interface eliminates drive shaft failures under heavy loads and simplifies service when needed.

COVERAGE & PRODUCTIVITY
The twin non-overlap six-blade rotors deliver a 10-foot (117 in) path width — fewer passes across the slab, faster cycle times, and more consistent results on warehouse floors, manufacturing areas, distribution centers, and big-box retail installations. Ergonomic seating and controls reduce operator fatigue over long finishing shifts.

OPERATOR CONTROLS
Active Speed Control lets operators precisely set and maintain target rotor RPM via a digital interface. The Visual Pitch Indicator displays blade angle on-screen for repeatable panning and burnishing settings. The digital display gives at-a-glance data on blade pitch, rotor speed, fuel selection, fuel level, engine speed, cruise control, and critical engine alerts. LED lighting supports indoor and low-light finishing operations. A retardant spray system provides uniform slab coverage via steering handle controls. Rotor Jog allows safe repositioning without surface marring. Additional operator amenities include a USB accessory outlet and cup holder.

DESIGN
The redesigned welded chassis features a full walk-around deck for maintenance access. An all-aluminum deep-skirted cylinder block provides rigidity, and the 'Set for Life' valve train minimizes engine maintenance requirements. The EPA-compliant combination catalytic converter/muffler system is standard.

IDEAL APPLICATIONS
Best suited for large commercial or industrial slabs — warehouses, manufacturing facilities, distribution centers, and retail pours — where finish quality, coverage speed, and uptime are mission-critical. Contractors doing high-volume or repeat slab work will maximize the machine's ROI.

MAINTENANCE & SUPPORT
CTA Services provides delivery, operator orientation, hydraulic system checks, blade pitch calibration support, and scheduled maintenance programs across the Charlotte metro. Contact us for current sale pricing, rental availability, and fleet service agreements.

specs.Engine: 84 HP Ford Power Products, Dual Fuel (Gas/LPG)
specs.Drive System: Hydrostatic — variable displacement pump & radial piston motors
specs.Controls: Digital display, Active Speed Control, Visual Pitch Indicator
specs.Spray System: Retardant spray via steering handle controls

shortDescription: Allen Engineering ROP180-P: Dedicated ride-on floor polishing machine with planetary head configuration, suited for large-format polished concrete and densified industrial floors.
longDescription: The Allen Engineering ROP180-P is a ride-on polishing platform designed for production-scale polished concrete programs. Its planetary head configuration applies consistent downward pressure across the polishing path, enabling uniform scratch removal and gloss development on large industrial, commercial, and warehouse floors. CTA Services LLC supports polished concrete programs with equipment, pads, densifiers, and on-call service across the Concord–Charlotte area. Ask about our rental program for single-project or ongoing polishing contracts.

name: Walk-Behind Power Trowel — 36 Inch (Gas)
shortDescription: 36-inch walk-behind gas power trowel: Workhorse finishing machine for residential slabs, smaller commercial pours, and tight access areas requiring blade or pan floating.
longDescription: A 36-inch walk-behind power trowel is the standard entry point for professional concrete finishing on pours from 1,000 to 5,000 sq ft. Single-rotor design delivers excellent maneuverability and blade or pan compatibility for floating and finishing passes. CTA Services LLC rents walk-behind trowels by the day, week, or month, with delivery to job sites across Cabarrus, Mecklenburg, and surrounding counties. Ask about our trowel blade and pan kits available with every rental.

name: Husqvarna PG 510 Concrete Grinder
shortDescription: Husqvarna PG 510: Single-phase 220V planetary concrete grinder for coating removal, surface prep, and concrete polishing on medium commercial floors.
longDescription: The Husqvarna PG 510 is a compact planetary grinder suited for surface preparation, epoxy coating removal, and polished concrete programs on floors from 500 to 10,000 sq ft. Its three planetary satellite heads deliver consistent grinding contact across flat and slightly uneven substrates. CTA Services LLC supplies, services, and rents Husqvarna concrete grinding equipment. Ask about compatible diamond tooling, wet/dry dust collection packages, and planned maintenance.
